objective: Bootstrap the next issue-backed child under issue #607 for bounded planning-first authority enforcement on governance execution validation and gate wiring only.
scope_boundary: Owns the planning-first authority enforcement slice only: scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, .github/workflows/governance-check.yml, tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/hldpro-governance.yml, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts.
Must prove blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority behavior without widening into broader local-hook, fallback-propagation, or consumer-verifier lanes.
Keeps #612, #614, #615, and blocked child #631 as explicit external boundaries.
Starts by producing the issue-local planning packet and validation evidence in this isolated worktree.
out_of_scope: Downstream hldpro-sim consumer verifier or drift-gate work under issue #614.
Broad standards rewrite unless strictly required for this bounded gate.
Any claim of repo-wide governance closure from this child alone.
research_summary: Parent issue #607 is planning-complete but still lacks the bounded implementation child that machine-enforces blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority behavior. Specialist review concluded the next clean move is a narrow #607 child on execution-scope validation plus governance/local-ci gate wiring only, because #612 is currently blocked behind #631 and #615's remaining residual is broader. The lane must keep #612, #614, #615, and #631 as external boundaries and return independent regression/impact proof requirements rather than prose-only closure.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and execution-scope boundaries before implementation changes.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, handoff, review packet, review placeholder, cross-review note, validation artifact, and closeout placeholder.
Restate the exact owned planning-authority surfaces confirmed by specialist review.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet is validated and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the exact planning-authority owned surfaces.
The packet keeps #612, #614, #615, and #631 as explicit external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
The packet states that implementation is blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.
The packet requires future implementation proof of blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority behavior with independent evidence.

sprints.name: Implementation Slice
sprints.goal: Implement the bounded planning-first authority enforcement path.
tasks: Tighten scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py so governance execution claims fail closed when planning authority is missing or invalid on the owned path.
Wire the bounded gate expectations into .github/workflows/governance-check.yml and tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/hldpro-governance.yml without reopening local-hook surfaces.
Return focused blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority tests plus independent regression/impact proof references.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation stays inside scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, .github/workflows/governance-check.yml, tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/hldpro-governance.yml, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
The implementation returns concrete blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority proof with exact commands, exit behavior, and validator or gate evidence.
The implementation requires downstream execution claims to cite independent regression/impact proof artifacts rather than prose-only attestation.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the next clean parent graph after the #616/#631 block and confirm the narrowest valid #607 child implementation surface.
specialist_reviews.summary: The next clean move is a new #607 child, not another #612 or #615 child. The owned surfaces must stay limited to assert_execution_scope.py, governance-check.yml, local-ci-gate profile wiring, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts. Residual: #616/#631 remain blocked and should not be widened from this lane.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the planning-only bootstrap packet shape, explicit external boundaries, and proof obligations for later implementation.
specialist_reviews.summary: The lane must not reopen #612, #614, or #615 and must not claim broader governance closure. It should require independent proof of blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority behavior before any future closure claim. Residual: packet review and validator replay must complete before implementation promotion.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review accepted the planning-only packet with follow-up to patch the PDCAR #631 boundary mention, replay validator results into the validation artifact, and preserve a one-line residual explanation for mixed specialist reviews before implementation promotion.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #632 is authorized for the bounded planning-authority tightening slice only: scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, focused tests, governance gate/profile proof replay, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the bounded planner-authority tightening so planning-only scopes cannot authorize implementation-shaped governance diffs, then return blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority proof from assert_execution_scope.py plus the wired local/CI gate path.
blocked_on: Issue #612 remains the degraded-fallback enforcement lane and blocked child #631 remains outside this lane.
Issue #615 remains the local pre-hook/startup/pretool/root-parity enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the hldpro-sim verifier and drift-gate lane.
Do not widen into hooks, startup/pretool/root-parity, degraded-fallback propagation, or hldpro-sim verifier work.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #632 remains bounded to scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py, .github/workflows/governance-check.yml, tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/hldpro-governance.yml, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
AC2: The lane preserves #612, #614, #615, and #631 as explicit external boundaries and does not absorb their owned enforcement surfaces.
AC3: No implementation may begin until the issue-local planning packet validates and sanctioned alternate-family review is recorded.

AC4: Future implementation proof must require concrete blocked-before-authority and allowed-after-authority evidence plus independent regression/impact proof references rather than prose-only closure.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit to hooks/ or .claude/settings.json from issue #632 is a material deviation because those surfaces belong to issue #615 children.
Any edit to scripts/orchestrator/packet_queue.py or fallback-log schema/writer surfaces from issue #632 is a material deviation because those belong to issue #612 children.
Any edit to hldpro-sim consumer verifier surfaces from issue #632 is a material deviation because those belong to issue #614.
Any implementation of planning-authority enforcement before the issue-local planning packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded is a material deviation.
Any claim that issue #632 closes broader governance enforcement outside the bounded planning-authority slice is a material deviation.
